question: Identify the NYC high school that was in the top 3 by overall progress score in both the 2011-12 and 2012-13 school years and had an A grade in the 2010-11 Progress Report. Find its ZIP code. Then compare the 2010 African American population percentage of the neighborhood whose primary ZIP Code matches that ZIP with that of the alphabetically last neighborhood among the neighborhoods to its south. How many percentage points higher was the ZIP-matching neighborhood? Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2011-2012-high-school-progress-report/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
import re

school_col = "School Name"
overall_col = "Overall Score"

filtered = df.copy()
if "School Type" in filtered.columns:
    filtered = filtered[filtered["School Type"].astype(str).str.contains("High", case=False, na=False)]

filtered[overall_col] = pd.to_numeric(filtered[overall_col], errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered.sort_values(overall_col, ascending=False)

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s

answer = filtered[school_col].dropna().astype(str).head(3).map(normalize_school).tolist()
1.subquestion: Which NYC high schools were in the top 3 by overall score in 2011-12?
answer: Academy for Careers in Television and Film

3.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2013-2014-doe-high-school-performance-directory/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
node_1_answer = ['It Takes a Village Academy', 'Manhattan Village Academy', 'Academy for Careers in Television and Film']
node_2_answer = ['It Takes a Village Academy', 'Academy for Careers in Television and Film', 'Brooklyn International High School']

import os
import io
import re

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s.lower()

candidate_names = sorted(set(node_1_answer) & set(node_2_answer))
candidate_norm_to_name = {normalize_school(x): str(x).strip() for x in candidate_names}

lookup_source = "datagov/2012-2013-high-school-progress-report/files/rows.txt"
lookup_local = lookup_source if os.path.exists(lookup_source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + lookup_source
if os.path.exists(lookup_local):
    lookup_df = pd.read_csv(lookup_local, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=lookup_source)
    lookup_df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)

lookup_df["_norm_name"] = lookup_df["School Name"].astype(str).map(normalize_school)
name_to_dbn = {
    row["_norm_name"]: str(row["DBN"]).strip()
    for _, row in lookup_df.iterrows()
    if row["_norm_name"] in candidate_norm_to_name
}

candidate_dbns = set(name_to_dbn.values())
filtered = df[
    df["DBN"].astype(str).str.strip().isin(candidate_dbns)
    & df["Progress_Rpt_10-11"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper().eq("A")
]

dbns = filtered["DBN"].astype(str).str.strip().tolist()
answer = [candidate_norm_to_name[norm_name] for norm_name, dbn in name_to_dbn.items() if dbn in dbns]
3.subquestion: Which schools from <intersection of node_1, node_2> had an A grade in the 2010-11 Progress Report?

4.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2012-2013-school-locations/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
node_3_answer = ['It Takes a Village Academy']

import re

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s.lower()

target = node_3_answer[0] if isinstance(node_3_answer, list) and node_3_answer else node_3_answer
target_norm = normalize_school(target)

df["_norm_loc"] = df["LOCATION_NAME"].astype(str).map(normalize_school)
row = df[df["_norm_loc"].eq(target_norm)].iloc[0]
loc_text = str(row.get("Location 1", ""))
match = re.search(r"\b(\d{5})(?:-\d{4})?\b", loc_text)
if match:
    answer = match.group(1)
else:
    addr_text = str(row.get("PRIMARY_ADDRESS_LINE_1", ""))
    match2 = re.search(r"\b(\d{5})(?:-\d{4})?\b", addr_text)
    answer = match2.group(1) if match2 else ""
4.subquestion: What is the ZIP code of <node_3 answer>?

5.fact:
East Flatbush is a residential neighborhood in the New York City borough of Brooklyn. East Flatbush is bounded by Crown Heights and Empire Boulevard to the north; Brownsville and East 98th Street to the east; Flatlands, Canarsie and the Long Island Rail Road's Bay Ridge Branch to the south; and the neighborhood of Flatbush and New York Avenue to the west. East Flatbush is a predominantly black neighborhood of African American and Afro-Caribbeans  and has a population of 135,619 as of the 2010 United States census.

East Flatbush is part of Brooklyn Community District 17, and its primary ZIP Code is 11203. It is patrolled by the 67th Precinct of the New York City Police Department. Politically it is represented by the New York City Council's 40th, 41st, and 45th Districts."Current City Council Districts for Kings County", New York City. Accessed May 5, 2017.

The racial makeup of the neighborhood was 1.3% (1,816) White, 88.7% (120,231) African American, 0.3% (366) Native American, 1.1% (1,480) Asian, 0% (45) Pacific Islander, 0.4% (523) from other races, and 1.6% (2,140) from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 6.6% (9,018) of the population.Table PL-P3A NTA: Total Population by Mutually Exclusive Race and Hispanic Origin - New York City Neighborhood Tabulation Areas*, 2010, Population Division - New York City Department of City Planning, March 29, 2011. Accessed June 14, 2016.
5.subquestion: For the neighborhood whose primary ZIP Code is <node_4 answer>, what was its 2010 African American share, and which neighborhood comes last alphabetically among the neighborhoods to its south?

Hispanic or Latino of any race were 8.9% (5,753) of the population.Table PL-P3A NTA: Total Population by Mutually Exclusive Race and Hispanic Origin - New York City Neighborhood Tabulation Areas*, 2010 , Population Division - New York City Department of City Planning, March 29, 2011. Accessed June 14, 2016.
